rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative tandem cyclization of olefinic carbonyls (1,3-dicarbonyls and N-allylamides) with diselenides to form seleno-dihydrofurans and seleno-oxazolines, constructing C-Se and C-O bonds simultaneously
electrochemical_role: Cathode reduces diaryl diselenide to a [RSeSeR]•- radical anion that fragments into ArSe• and ArSe-; the selenyl radical adds to the alkene, and the resulting C-radical is oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that is trapped intramolecularly by the enol oxygen

key_intermediates.name: alkyl C-radical from PhSe• addition to alkenyl carbonyl (intermediate II)
alkyl carbocation after anodic oxidation of II

electron_flow: At the cathode, ArSeSeAr accepts one electron to form a radical anion that dissociates into ArSe• and ArSe-; ArSe• adds across the alkene of the olefinic carbonyl giving a β-selenyl C-radical, which is oxidized at the anode to a cation, then intramolecular O-attack by the enol oxygen and deprotonation deliver the dihydrofuran (or oxazoline). Protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 0.6 mmol (2 equiv) TEMPO suppressed the reaction; only trace 3ba detected, supporting radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl diselenide (CV study)
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclic voltammetry showed oxidation at +1.8 V and reduction at -1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl, indicating PhSeSePh can participate in both anodic and cathodic processes

notes: Authors propose two pathways: path (a, favored) involves cathodic reduction of PhSeSePh to PhSe• which adds to the alkene, followed by anodic oxidation of the resulting C-radical; path (b) cannot be fully ruled out — direct anodic oxidation of 1b first to an enol/α-carbonyl radical that is then attacked by PhSe•/PhSeSePh. H2 evolution at the cathode is invoked to balance the redox.
